channel.title: briefUpdatesMainNLbusinessAndMarkets
channel.description: This is a randomly ordered feed of the most recent entries from briefUpdatesMainNLbusinessAndMarkets, skipping any feeds with errors.

item.title: Olympic runner, Mo Farah has a message for struggling Gen Z: ‘I was a child trafficked, but I never gave up on myself’
item.description: From human trafficking survivor to Olympic champion, Mo Farah has a message for Gen Z: life will knock you down, but your success is your responsibili...

item.title: Sam Altman gets defensive about AI’s massive electricity usage: ‘It also takes a lot of energy to train a human’
item.description: Altman said data centers have moved past water-intensive cooling, but 56% globally still use evaporative systems.

item.title: Discord cuts ties with Peter Thiel-backed verification software after its code was found tied to U.S. surveillance efforts
item.description: Discord cut ties with its age-verification partner after exposed code fueled federal-reporting concerns, months after a breach hit 70,000 users.

item.title: This Expert’s Viral Substack Predicts Which White‑Collar Jobs AI Will Erase Next
item.description: A finance writer’s dystopian 2028 thought experiment argues that AI will soon take over in dramatic ways.

item.title: These saunas and spas for frogs could bring a species back from the brink of extinction
item.description: At a park near Canberra, Australia, a series of small white pyramid-shaped boxes are part of a new experiment: Can “frog saunas” help bring back an en...

item.title: Scientists are pushing back on warnings that microplastics damage your health, saying people are just obese and calling some studies ‘a joke’
item.description: Some scientists are saying previous studies warning of the damage microplastics may cause are exaggerated and are false positives triggered by human f...

item.title: Oprah Winfrey, Warren Buffett, Mark Cuban, and Steve Jobs Each Define Success Differently. You Should, Too
item.description: Want to feel more fulfilled, satisfied, and happy? Define ‘success’ your way, and then work to achieve it.

item.title: AI Was the Reason to Buy Stocks. Now It’s the Reason to Sell
item.description: AI has flipped from the market’s biggest tailwind to its newest systemic risk.

item.title: How to navigate a boss who keeps changing priorities
item.description: Three weeks into her new role as VP of operations, “Maria” got an 11:47 p.m. Slack from her COO: “Where are we on the Q3 supply chain numbers?” She ha...

item.title: Amazon’s CEO just made a scary prediction for 2026. Economists worry he’s right
item.description: Last year was full of talk about tariffs. Are they coming up or going down ? On which products and countries? How could businesses handle all the unce...

item.title: ‘I thought about folding’: How limited healthcare access cripples solopreneurs
item.description: Roger Sauerhaft thought he had done everything right. The 38-year-old PR consultant had been running his solo practice in New York since 2021, paying ...

item.title: Singapore’s Singtel partners with Nvidia to build a research lab for companies that care about data sovereignty
item.description: Singtel is leaning into its AI and data center business as its mobile services business faces more competition.

item.title: Without Saying a Word, Olympic Skater Ilia Malinin Taught a Stunning Lesson About Success
item.description: Here’s what most people don’t realize.

item.title: Trump’s plan to circumvent European internet content bans is a geopolitical nightmare
item.description: A year ago at the Munich Security Conference, Vice President JD Vance accused Europe of using “ugly, Soviet-era words like misinformation and disinfor...

item.title: Dave’s Hot Chicken CEO Sold for $1 Billion. Now He’s Betting on a New Startup
item.description: Mike’s Red Tacos, a new franchised birria taco business, is taking a page from the Dave’s Hot Chicken playbook.

item.title: What airlines, hotels and Airbnb actually owe travelers stranded in Mexico
PayPal shares pop on takeover hopes. Here’s who could swoop in with a purchase.
Did a blog post cause software stocks to lose more than $200B in market cap?
Where Trump’s affordability ideas stand ahead of his State of the Union
Dow drops over 800 points, stocks close sharply lower on tariff tumult, AI fears
